name: Jira
description: Search issues with JQL, view and update tickets, manage projects, track sprints, and access development info (commits, PRs) in Jira Cloud through AI chat.
setup_notes:
Requires an Atlassian Cloud site name, user email, and API token.

Admin setup:
1. Go to https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens
2. Click 'Create API token' and give it a descriptive label (e.g., 'MCP Server')
3. Copy the generated token immediately (it won't be shown again)
4. Determine your site name: it's the subdomain of your Jira URL (e.g., for https://mycompany.atlassian.net, the site name is 'mycompany')
5. Enter the site name, the email address associated with the Atlassian account, and the API token in the server configuration

Note: This server only supports Jira Cloud (not Jira Server/Data Center). Atlassian also offers an official remote MCP server at https://mcp.atlassian.com with OAuth 2.1 authentication, but it requires dynamic client registration support. This template uses the community @aashari/mcp-server-atlassian-jira package for simpler API token-based setup.
